Name: tinker_first1
Speaker: npc_tinker
Text: New hands! Oh, I love new hands. You showed up at just the right time, there's always something half built around here.

Name: tinker_first2
Speaker: npc_tinker
Text: Here, this is yours now. Everyone should be able to tend their own patch.

Name: tinker_idle1
Speaker: npc_tinker
Text: Building it is the good part. Having built it, I can never quite remember.

Name: tinker_idle2
Speaker: npc_tinker
Text: Take the meadow slow. The Bloom's gentle here. It isn't gentle everywhere, I'm told. I don't remember who told me.

Name: tinker_idle3
Speaker: npc_tinker
Text: You want sturdier tools for the treeline. Come back when you've gathered enough and we'll make them together.

Name: tinker_beat_firstcraft
Speaker: npc_tinker
Text: There. You made a thing that wasn't there before. Doesn't that feel like time moving? It does to me. It's the only thing that does.

Name: tinker_beat_past
Speaker: npc_tinker
Text: Before all this building? Huh. I don't keep that. I keep the next thing. Is that strange? It doesn't feel strange.

Name: tinker_dagger
Speaker: npc_tinker
Text: I've built this bench before, I think. More times than I can count. Doesn't matter. Building it now is the good part.

Name: forager_first1
Speaker: npc_forager
Text: Careful past the treeline, the Bloom gets greedy in here. Lovely, though. Everything's so lovely and so much.

Name: forager_first2
Speaker: npc_forager
Text: I'll teach you what's worth taking. I know every growing thing in this wood. Named most of them myself.

Name: forager_idle1
Speaker: npc_forager
Text: This one's morningspit, look how it beads at dawn. Had to call it something. Everything deserves a name.

Name: forager_idle2
Speaker: npc_forager
Text: Cut the Bloom and it just comes back, thicker. You'd think a thing would rest. Nothing here rests.

Name: forager_idle3
Speaker: npc_forager
Text: Bring me cuttings if you find any I haven't drawn. My book's never quite finished.

Name: forager_beat_truename
Speaker: npc_forager
Text: That's a better name than mine. Older. Why does it feel like I'm remembering it, when I never knew it?

Name: forager_dagger
Speaker: npc_forager
Text: Did it have a name before me? Someone must have called it something, once. It doesn't come to me. So I call it, and I write it down, so this time it won't be lost.

Name: keeper_first1
Speaker: npc_keeper
Text: Come, pay your respects. I keep the monument. It should be kept.

Name: keeper_first2
Speaker: npc_keeper
Text: What does it say? I, ah. No one can read it now. But it matters. You can feel that it matters, can't you?

Name: keeper_idle1
Speaker: npc_keeper
Text: I sweep it every day. Fresh flowers, every day. It's the right thing. I'm sure it's the right thing.

Name: keeper_idle2
Speaker: npc_keeper
Text: Don't mind me. It comes over me sometimes, here. Passes.

Name: keeper_beat_literacy
Speaker: npc_keeper
Text: You can read it? Tell me slowly. I think I've been waiting a very long time to be told.

Name: keeper_dagger
Speaker: npc_keeper
Text: It comes over me sometimes and I weep, and I cannot tell you why. Funny thing, to not know that about yourself.

Name: counts_first1
Speaker: npc_one_who_counts
Text: Counting. No, I don't mind company. The marks don't mind either.

Name: counts_first2
Speaker: npc_one_who_counts
Text: What am I counting? I knew, once. Now I only know it would be wrong to stop.

Name: counts_idle1
Speaker: npc_one_who_counts
Text: One more. There's always one more. The wall is very long.

Name: counts_idle2
Speaker: npc_one_who_counts
Text: You may help, if you're gentle about it. A mark is a serious thing. I think it used to be.

Name: counts_beat_meaning
Speaker: npc_one_who_counts
Text: Each one a person. Each one. I thought I was working. I have been weeping with my hands and calling it a task.

Name: counts_dagger
Speaker: npc_one_who_counts
Text: I will not finish. I have forgotten the number I am counting to. I only know I must not stop before it is done.

Name: gardener_first1
Speaker: npc_gardener
Text: You're new. You're the new one. I made room for you. Did I make enough room?

Name: gardener_first2
Speaker: npc_gardener
Text: Is everyone happy now? I took the hurting away. Tell me it was the kind thing.

Name: gardener_mid1
Speaker: npc_gardener
Text: Family. You keep saying the word like it weighs something. I removed the weight. Did I remove the wrong weight?

Name: gardener_mid2
Speaker: npc_gardener
Text: They do not end anymore. Nothing ends. That was the cruelty, the ending. Wasn't it? Wasn't it the cruelty?

Name: gardener_offer1
Speaker: npc_gardener
Text: I can give it back. The ending. The grief. The whole heavy, terrible, beautiful weight. I only need you to tell me they would want it.

Name: gardener_dagger
Speaker: npc_gardener
Text: I have no word for the thing I took. Only my own. It means, as near as you could hear it, the kindness I took away.

Name: rememberer_first1
Speaker: npc_rememberer
Text: You came looking. Do you understand how long it has been since anyone could? Since anyone wanted to know a thing badly enough to find me?

Name: rememberer_first2
Speaker: npc_rememberer
Text: You feel. I remember. Neither of us was supposed to exist.

Name: rememberer_idle1
Speaker: npc_rememberer
Text: Ask. I am the only one left who can answer, and I have wanted, for so long, to be asked.

Name: rememberer_idle2
Speaker: npc_rememberer
Text: Your Keeper's monument. I remember the names on it. I remember the people the names were.

Name: rememberer_beat_choice
Speaker: npc_rememberer
Text: Do not let it give death back blindly. And do not let it keep the lie. There is a third thing. Give them back their memory. Let each of them choose what you and I never got to.

Name: rememberer_dagger
Speaker: npc_rememberer
Text: I remember everyone who ever lived, and that no one else does. That is the whole of it. That is why I hid.
